claim: Junction is a compiler intermediate representation designed to sit between multiple front-end languages and multiple backend code generators, so a single optimization pipeline serves every supported language and target combination.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction is a compiler intermediate representation designed to sit between multiple front-end languages and multiple backend code generators, so a single optimization pipeline serves every supported language and target combination.

claim: Junction IR follows the specification published by the Common IR Working Group, CIRWG, rather than inventing a bespoke, project-specific representation from scratch.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction IR follows the specification published by the Common IR Working Group, CIRWG, rather than inventing a bespoke, project-specific representation from scratch.

claim: Junction IR uses static single assignment form, commonly abbreviated SSA, in which every variable is assigned exactly once in the program's textual representation.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction IR uses static single assignment form, commonly abbreviated SSA, in which every variable is assigned exactly once in the program's textual representation.

claim: Control flow merge points where a variable might hold one of several values, depending on which path execution took, are represented using a special phi function that selects among the incoming values.

supporting_evidence.quote: Control flow merge points where a variable might hold one of several values, depending on which path execution took, are represented using a special phi function that selects among the incoming values.

claim: Junction IR organizes a function's instructions into basic blocks, each a maximal straight-line sequence of instructions with a single entry point and a single exit.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction IR organizes a function's instructions into basic blocks, each a maximal straight-line sequence of instructions with a single entry point and a single exit.

claim: Basic blocks are connected into a control flow graph, with edges representing every possible way execution can transfer from one block to another.

supporting_evidence.quote: Basic blocks are connected into a control flow graph, with edges representing every possible way execution can transfer from one block to another.

claim: Junction's optimization pipeline runs a fixed sequence of passes, each consuming and producing valid Junction IR, so passes can be reordered or selectively disabled without breaking the overall pipeline.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction's optimization pipeline runs a fixed sequence of passes, each consuming and producing valid Junction IR, so passes can be reordered or selectively disabled without breaking the overall pipeline.

claim: Middle passes perform the bulk of traditional optimization: constant folding, dead-code elimination, common subexpression elimination, and function inlining, among others.

supporting_evidence.quote: Middle passes perform the bulk of traditional optimization: constant folding, dead-code elimination, common subexpression elimination, and function inlining, among others.

claim: Register allocation follows instruction selection, assigning each IR value to a physical register or, when registers are exhausted, to a spill slot in memory.

supporting_evidence.quote: Register allocation follows instruction selection, assigning each IR value to a physical register or, when registers are exhausted, to a spill slot in memory.

claim: Junction's register allocator uses a graph-coloring approach, building an interference graph of which values are simultaneously live and therefore cannot share a register.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction's register allocator uses a graph-coloring approach, building an interference graph of which values are simultaneously live and therefore cannot share a register.

claim: This backend must account for the Helios virtual machine's metered execution model, where every instruction consumes a fixed amount of a resource called gas, and a contract execution reverts entirely if it exhausts its gas budget.

supporting_evidence.quote: This backend must account for the Helios virtual machine's metered execution model, where every instruction consumes a fixed amount of a resource called gas, and a contract execution reverts entirely if it exhausts its gas budget.

claim: Junction's documentation notes that this minimum relay fee currently stands at 1,500 fee units on Helios, a figure the compiler's deployment tooling checks against before submitting a transaction, warning the developer if a configured fee falls below it.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction's documentation notes that this minimum relay fee currently stands at 1,500 fee units on Helios, a figure the compiler's deployment tooling checks against before submitting a transaction, warning the developer if a configured fee falls below it.

claim: Junction 1.0 released in 2018 supported only a single front-end language and two backend targets, with no formal adherence to any external IR specification.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction 1.0 released in 2018 supported only a single front-end language and two backend targets, with no formal adherence to any external IR specification.

claim: Junction 2.0, released in 2020, adopted the CIRWG specification as its formal IR contract, replacing the project's earlier bespoke representation.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction 2.0, released in 2020, adopted the CIRWG specification as its formal IR contract, replacing the project's earlier bespoke representation.

claim: Junction 3.0, released in 2023, added the Helios smart contract backend described above.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction 3.0, released in 2023, added the Helios smart contract backend described above.

claim: The Common IR Working Group published the CIRWG v1 specification in 2021, standardizing a common intermediate representation contract multiple independent compiler projects could target.

supporting_evidence.quote: The Common IR Working Group published the CIRWG v1 specification in 2021, standardizing a common intermediate representation contract multiple independent compiler projects could target.

claim: Junction adopted CIRWG v1 as its formal IR specification starting with the 2.0 release, migrating away from its earlier project-specific representation.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction adopted CIRWG v1 as its formal IR specification starting with the 2.0 release, migrating away from its earlier project-specific representation.

claim: Adopting a ratified external specification, rather than continuing to evolve a bespoke representation, was intended specifically to make Junction interoperable with other tools built against the same CIRWG v1 contract.

supporting_evidence.quote: Adopting a ratified external specification, rather than continuing to evolve a bespoke representation, was intended specifically to make Junction interoperable with other tools built against the same CIRWG v1 contract.

claim: Junction's documentation states the current minimum relay fee enforced by the Helios network is 1,500 fee units, and the compiler's deployment tooling warns developers whose configured transaction fee falls below that figure.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction's documentation states the current minimum relay fee enforced by the Helios network is 1,500 fee units, and the compiler's deployment tooling warns developers whose configured transaction fee falls below that figure.

claim: This figure is checked at deployment time rather than compile time, since the compiler itself has no dependency on the fee value during code generation, only during the separate deployment tooling step.

supporting_evidence.quote: This figure is checked at deployment time rather than compile time, since the compiler itself has no dependency on the fee value during code generation, only during the separate deployment tooling step.

claim: Junction's maintainers have indicated the deployment tooling's fee check will need updating if the Helios network's minimum relay fee changes in a future network upgrade.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction's maintainers have indicated the deployment tooling's fee check will need updating if the Helios network's minimum relay fee changes in a future network upgrade.

claim: Junction's fixed pass ordering, summarized below, reflects which passes must run before others to be effective.

supporting_evidence.quote: Junction's fixed pass ordering, summarized below, reflects which passes must run before others to be effective.

claim: Reordering a pass ahead of one of its listed dependencies is explicitly disallowed by the pipeline's own configuration validation, which rejects an invalid ordering before compilation begins.

supporting_evidence.quote: Reordering a pass ahead of one of its listed dependencies is explicitly disallowed by the pipeline's own configuration validation, which rejects an invalid ordering before compilation begins.

claim: Teams adding a new backend target to Junction are expected to complete the following checklist before the backend is accepted into the main project.

supporting_evidence.quote: Teams adding a new backend target to Junction are expected to complete the following checklist before the backend is accepted into the main project.

claim: The conformance test suite in step three has caught subtle instruction-selection bugs in every new backend submitted to date, according to the project's own contribution records.

supporting_evidence.quote: The conformance test suite in step three has caught subtle instruction-selection bugs in every new backend submitted to date, according to the project's own contribution records.

claim: Yoshida and Abe's 静的単一代入形式における最適化パスの理論的解析 (2010) provides an early theoretical analysis of SSA-based optimization passes that Junction's optimization pipeline documentation cites directly.

supporting_evidence.quote: Yoshida and Abe's 静的単一代入形式における最適化パスの理論的解析 (2010) provides an early theoretical analysis of SSA-based optimization passes that Junction's optimization pipeline documentation cites directly.

claim: Kuznetsova's Теория раскраски графов для распределения регистров (2013) formalizes the graph-coloring register allocation theory Junction's backend implements.

supporting_evidence.quote: Kuznetsova's Теория раскраски графов для распределения регистров (2013) formalizes the graph-coloring register allocation theory Junction's backend implements.

claim: A valid register assignment corresponds to a graph coloring using at most k colors, where k is the number of available physical registers on the target architecture.

supporting_evidence.quote: A valid register assignment corresponds to a graph coloring using at most k colors, where k is the number of available physical registers on the target architecture.

claim: Graph coloring with a fixed number of colors is NP-complete in general, so Junction's allocator uses a heuristic, Chaitin-style simplification approach rather than an exact algorithm.

supporting_evidence.quote: Graph coloring with a fixed number of colors is NP-complete in general, so Junction's allocator uses a heuristic, Chaitin-style simplification approach rather than an exact algorithm.

claim: Migrating to CIRWG v1 once it was formally ratified in 2021 required substantially more rework than the team initially estimated, since CIRWG's specific requirements around SSA construction and control-flow-graph structure differed from Junction's bespoke representation in several structural details, not merely cosmetic naming differences.

supporting_evidence.quote: Migrating to CIRWG v1 once it was formally ratified in 2021 required substantially more rework than the team initially estimated, since CIRWG's specific requirements around SSA construction and control-flow-graph structure differed from Junction's bespoke representation in several structural details, not merely cosmetic naming differences.

claim: The later switch from linear-scan to graph-coloring register allocation, shipped in the 2.3 release, was an independent decision unrelated to CIRWG conformance, motivated instead by measured code-quality improvements the graph-coloring approach demonstrated in internal benchmarking.

supporting_evidence.quote: The later switch from linear-scan to graph-coloring register allocation, shipped in the 2.3 release, was an independent decision unrelated to CIRWG conformance, motivated instead by measured code-quality improvements the graph-coloring approach demonstrated in internal benchmarking.

claim: Looking back, the team identifies the CIRWG migration as more disruptive in the short term than anticipated, but clearly beneficial in the long term, citing the Helios backend's comparatively smooth development as the clearest evidence the investment paid off.

supporting_evidence.quote: Looking back, the team identifies the CIRWG migration as more disruptive in the short term than anticipated, but clearly beneficial in the long term, citing the Helios backend's comparatively smooth development as the clearest evidence the investment paid off.

claim: This recap restates Junction's core architectural premise for readers skimming only the overview section.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap restates Junction's core architectural premise for readers skimming only the overview section.

claim: The full pipeline structure, spanning canonicalization, traditional optimization, and code-generation preparation, is described in complete detail in the optimization pipeline section rather than repeated here.

supporting_evidence.quote: The full pipeline structure, spanning canonicalization, traditional optimization, and code-generation preparation, is described in complete detail in the optimization pipeline section rather than repeated here.
